description: Cards positioned freely on a canvas with (x, y) coordinates. Users drag cards to arrange.
cons: Harder to implement drag + persistence than a grid

description: When cards have no position, lay them in a responsive grid as initial state.
During drag, update client state immediately; on drop, persist to server. Revert on failure.
?theme=figma|miro|notion query parameter controls the active design theme via CSS variables.
pros: Easy to delete unused themes after pick
cons: Not persistent (resets on new tab) — acceptable for MVP

description: Server-side permission check: owner can do anything, editor can add/edit/move cards, viewer can only read.
cons: Need to inject a mock current user (query param or hardcoded) until auth lands
